seoTitle: How to Perform Sprint Planning in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to run sprint planning in Jira by prioritizing the backlog, estimating story points, assigning issues, and setting a sprint goal.
intro: Sprint planning sets the tone for the whole iteration, so getting it right in Jira matters. This guide covers prioritizing the product backlog, estimating story points, assigning issues, and setting a clear sprint goal before kicking things off.

steps.heading: Open the product backlog
steps.body: Navigate to your backlog view to see the full list of unprioritized and unscheduled work.

steps.heading: Prioritize issues and estimate stories
steps.body: Order the backlog by priority and add story point estimates so the team has a shared sense of effort for each item.

steps.heading: Create the sprint and set a goal
steps.body: Create a new sprint container and write a clear sprint goal that describes what the team is aiming to accomplish.

steps.heading: Start the sprint planning session
steps.body: Walk through the prioritized backlog with your team, pulling items into the sprint until you reach a realistic capacity.

faq.question: How do I run sprint planning in Jira?
faq.answer: Open the backlog, prioritize and estimate the issues, create a sprint with a clear goal, and pull in work until you reach team capacity.

faq.question: Who leads sprint planning sessions?
faq.answer: Scrum Masters typically facilitate sprint planning, with product owners setting priorities and the development team estimating effort.

faq.question: How do story points factor into sprint planning?
faq.answer: Story point estimates help the team gauge how much work realistically fits into the sprint based on past velocity.

faq.question: What is a sprint goal and why does it matter?
faq.answer: A sprint goal is a short statement of what the team aims to achieve during the sprint, keeping planning focused rather than just filling capacity.
